name: B.S. in Engineering Technology, Construction Management Technology

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: The plan of study as captured is incoherent for this program: only 3 of the expected 8 requirement groups were parsed (summedTermCredits 42 of a stated 120), and those three parsed terms list biomedical courses (BMET 320, BMET 415, BMET 440, BMET 450, MET 403, MNET 315/414/416/420) that belong to the Biomedical Engineering Technology program, not construction management. The genuine construction-management content (CET 313/314/317/322/411/413/415/416/421/423, CMT 332/436/452, CIM 205) appears only in the unordered sample pool, so a reader cannot follow any CM progression from the page.
dimensions.suggestion: Republish the complete, correct 8-term Construction Management Technology plan of study and remove the biomedical courses that have bled in from the BMET program.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: Among the genuine construction-management courses the prerequisite graph is sensible (deepest chain 7; CET 411 <- 'CET 313, CET 314, CET 317'; CMT 436 <- CMT 332; CET 416 <- CET 415), and only 5 dangling references (GER 200/300, PHYS 102A/103A, SET 200A) are placeholders. Confidence is undercut, however, by an anomalously high referencedCourses count of 74, indicating multiple programs' courses merged into this record.
dimensions.suggestion: After restoring the correct plan, prune the merged/biomedical course references so the prerequisite graph reflects only the CM curriculum.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes or objectives appear in the data; only a partial plan and course descriptions are present. This is a real omission independent of the parsing problems.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish explicit CM student outcomes (estimating and scheduling, contracts and safety, project delivery, structural/MEP systems literacy).

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: Credit accounting is broken: summedTermCredits is 42 against a stated 120 (creditReconcileDelta -78.0) with only 3 of 8 terms parsed, and those terms are contaminated with biomedical courses. Because this is a standalone B.S. (not a combined-degree or minor), the large negative delta is a genuine data/presentation defect, not merely a caveat, and a catalog reader cannot see the credit distribution at all.
dimensions.suggestion: Restore all eight terms so the plan sums to 120 credits and creditReconcileDelta returns to 0.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: The page is the weakest in the set: more than half the plan is missing, the visible terms show the wrong (biomedical) courses under a Construction Management title, and one prerequisite string on ECET 210 has the prerequisite text bleeding into the description ('PRE: None This is an introductory course ...'). A prospective student cannot use this page to understand the program.
dimensions.suggestion: Rebuild the catalog page from the correct CM plan of study and fix the ECET 210 prerequisite/description parsing artifact.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: Judged on the genuine CM course pool, the content is reasonably current: estimating (CET 411), project management (CET 415/416), contracts (CET 421), safety/OSHA (CET 423, IE 473), codes (CET 322), structural and MEP systems (CMT 332/452), temporary structures (CMT 436) and organizational behavior (HRM 301). It shares the discipline's modern gaps in BIM/VDC, lean construction and sustainability.
dimensions.suggestion: Modernize CET 317 toward BIM/VDC and add lean-construction and sustainable-building content once the plan is corrected.

descriptionQuality.justification: The sampled courses (including CET/CMT construction content) carry specific, informative descriptions (CET 411 on quantity takeoff, pricing and computerized estimating; CMT 452 on water supply, HVAC, fire-protection and electrical building systems; CMT 436 on formwork, falsework and shoring). Description quality is a bright spot amid the structural problems.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: Prerequisites among the construction courses are explicit and consistent (CET 411 <- 'CET 313, CET 314, CET 317'; CMT 436 <- CMT 332), but overall clarity is compromised by the merged 74-course reference set (biomedical courses mixed in) and the ECET 210 record where prerequisite text runs into the description.

missingTopics.rationale: CET 317 is general construction computing; there is no model-based coordination or 4D/5D scheduling course.

missingTopics.topic: Lean construction and advanced project controls
missingTopics.rationale: CET 415/411 are traditional; Last Planner, pull planning and integrated project delivery are absent.
CET 413 Environmental Science is the only environmental course; energy and LEED content is missing.

missingTopics.topic: Construction risk, finance and data analytics
missingTopics.rationale: FIN 315 is general corporate finance; construction-specific cost-risk and data analytics are not covered.
CMT 436 addresses temporary structures but offsite/modular delivery methods are not represented.

emergingTrends.trend: Digital twins and BIM-based project delivery
emergingTrends.rationale: Model-centric delivery is now standard for construction managers, the natural extension of CET 317.

emergingTrends.trend: Construction robotics, drones and reality capture
emergingTrends.rationale: Automated layout and drone-based progress monitoring are entering mainstream CM practice.
Offsite manufacturing is reshaping schedules and cost, extending CMT 436 content.

emergingTrends.trend: AI-driven scheduling, estimating and risk analytics
emergingTrends.rationale: Data-driven controls modernize CET 411/415 for the next decade of practice.
Owners increasingly require carbon reporting, which CET 413 could incorporate.

topSuggestions: Fix the plan of study: only 42 of 120 credits are parsed (delta -78) and the visible terms are contaminated with biomedical courses (BMET 320/415/440/450) that do not belong to Construction Management -- republish the full, correct 8-term CM plan.
Add BIM/VDC and lean-construction content and modernize CET 317/411/415.
Add sustainable-building/LEED and construction data-analytics content.
Resolve placeholder references (GER 200/300, PHYS 102A/103A, SET 200A) and the ECET 210 prerequisite/description parsing artifact.
